statement.markdown:
Given an integer array nums of size n, find the maximum XOR of two elements (or the maximum of just one element if n is odd) in the array.
A binary number is represented as a 32-bit integer. The input array contains only 32-bit integers. This problem can be solved by considering the XOR properties.
statement.inputFormat: The input file will contain multiple test cases, with each test case having n integers separated by space (1 <= n <= 100).
statement.outputFormat: For each test case, output the maximum XOR of two elements.
statement.notes: You can assume that input data is valid and there will be no extra spaces in input except the ones between numbers.

hints.text: Use Trie data structure to solve the problem.

editorial.title: Editorial
editorial.intuition: Start by representing numbers as binary strings. Then, create a Trie data structure and insert all binary numbers in the array into the Trie. Finally, return a maximum XOR which can be found by searching for the largest value that can be represented using two different paths in the Trie.
editorial.bruteForce: Try all possible pairs, calculate the XOR, and find the maximum value.
editorial.optimalSolution: Use Trie and binary representations to find maximum XOR.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: Trie allows efficient search for maximum XOR.
editorial.dryRun: Start by representing numbers 6 and 7 as binary strings: 6=110, 7=111. Insert these strings into a Trie and find the maximum XOR.
